label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to figure out if the sentence "Luke Voit was out at first." is plausible. Hmm, let me start by breaking it down. First, who is Luke Voit? I know he's a baseball player. He plays for the New York Yankees, I think. Right, he's a first baseman. So he usually plays first base. Now, the sentence says he was out at first. In baseball terms, that would mean that he was out while playing at first base. But wait, usually, when a player is out at first, it's a batter who is running to first base after hitting the ball. The batter is out if they don't reach first base before the throw or if they step on the bag and the umpire calls them out. But Luke Voit is a player who is fielding, not batting. So if he's at first base, he's the one who would be receiving the throw to put out a runner. So the idea that he was out at first base doesn't make sense because he's the fielder.
